name: Can I keep my existing interior trim when I install new windows?
headline: Can I keep my existing interior trim when I install new windows?

mainEntity.name: Can I keep my existing interior trim when I install new windows in my older home?
mainEntity.text: Can I keep my existing interior trim when I install new windows in my older home?

acceptedAnswer.text: For older homes in Pacifica, Daly City, and nearby areas, you can usually keep your interior trim if you choose **“insert” or “pocket” replacement windows** that fit into the existing frame. The installer removes the old sashes and hardware but leaves the main frame and trim in place. This helps maintain the original look of the home and can reduce the amount of repainting or patching needed. However, if the existing frame is damaged, out of square, or has rot, your installer may recommend replacing the frame and trim to fix the problem properly. In that case, the interior trim will need to be removed and replaced or recreated. A common pitfall is insisting on saving trim when the frame behind it is failing. That can hide problems and lead to future leaks or structural issues. On the other hand, some contractors might default to removing trim even when careful insert installation could preserve your home’s character. Talk clearly with your installer about your priorities for appearance and preservation. Ask whether they can match your existing trim style if replacement is necessary, and factor in the cost of repainting or staining. Knowing your options upfront helps you avoid surprises once the old windows come out.
